firewall.description: Controls if resources should be created
firewall.name: create

firewall.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
firewall.name: tags

firewall.description: A setting indicating whether the firewall is protected against changes to its Availability Zone configuration. When set to true, you must first disable this protection before adding or removing Availability Zones
firewall.name: availability_zone_change_protection

firewall.description: Set of types for which to collect analysis metrics. Valid values: `TLS_SNI`, `HTTP_HOST`. Defaults to `[]`
firewall.name: enabled_analysis_types

firewall.description: The ID of the transit gateway to which the firewall is attached. Required when creating a transit gateway-attached firewall
firewall.name: transit_gateway_id

firewall.description: Controls if a Logging Configuration should be created
firewall.name: create_logging_configuration

firewall.description: A list of min 1, max 2 configuration blocks describing the destination for the logging configuration
firewall.name: logging_configuration_destination_config

firewall.description: A boolean flag indicating whether it is possible to delete the firewall. Defaults to `true`
firewall.name: delete_protection

firewall.description: KMS encryption configuration settings
firewall.name: encryption_configuration

firewall.description: A friendly description of the firewall
firewall.name: description

firewall.description: A boolean flag indicating whether it is possible to change the associated firewall policy. Defaults to `false`
firewall.name: firewall_policy_change_protection

firewall.description: A friendly name of the firewall
firewall.name: name

firewall.description: Set of configuration blocks describing the public subnets. Each subnet must belong to a different Availability Zone in the VPC. AWS Network Firewall creates a firewall endpoint in each subnet
firewall.name: subnet_mapping

firewall.description: The unique identifier of the VPC where AWS Network Firewall should create the firewall
firewall.name: vpc_id

firewall.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
firewall.name: region

firewall.description: Required when creating a transit gateway-attached firewall. Set of configuration blocks describing the avaiability availability where you want to create firewall endpoints for a transit gateway-attached firewall
firewall.name: availability_zone_mapping

firewall.description: The ARN of the Firewall Policy to use
firewall.name: firewall_policy_arn

firewall.description: A boolean flag indicating whether it is possible to change the associated subnet(s). Defaults to `true`
firewall.name: subnet_change_protection

policy.description: Controls if resources should be created
policy.name: create

policy.description: A configuration block that defines options on how the policy handles stateful rules. See [Stateful Engine Options](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/resources/networkfirewall_firewall_policy#stateful-engine-options) for details
policy.name: stateful_engine_options

policy.description: Set of actions to take on a fragmented packet if it does not match any of the stateless rules in the policy. You must specify one of the standard actions including: `aws:drop`, `aws:pass`, or `aws:forward_to_sfe`
policy.name: stateless_fragment_default_actions

policy.description: A friendly name of the firewall policy
policy.name: name

policy.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
policy.name: tags

policy.description: Contains variables that you can use to override default Suricata settings in your firewall policy
policy.name: policy_variables

policy.description: Set of configuration blocks containing references to the stateful rule groups that are used in the policy. See [Stateful Rule Group Reference](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/resources/networkfirewall_firewall_policy#stateful-rule-group-reference) for details
policy.name: stateful_rule_group_reference

policy.description: Set of configuration blocks describing the custom action definitions that are available for use in the firewall policy's `stateless_default_actions`
policy.name: stateless_custom_action

policy.description: Set of actions to take on a packet if it does not match any of the stateless rules in the policy. You must specify one of the standard actions including: `aws:drop`, `aws:pass`, or `aws:forward_to_sfe`
policy.name: stateless_default_actions

policy.description: A list of IAM principals allowed in the resource policy
policy.name: resource_policy_principals

policy.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
policy.name: region

policy.description: A friendly description of the firewall policy
policy.name: description

policy.description: KMS encryption configuration settings
policy.name: encryption_configuration

policy.description: Set of configuration blocks containing references to the stateless rule groups that are used in the policy. See [Stateless Rule Group Reference](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/resources/networkfirewall_firewall_policy#stateless-rule-group-reference) for details
policy.name: stateless_rule_group_reference

policy.description: Controls if a resource policy should be created
policy.name: create_resource_policy

policy.description: Controls if a resource policy should be attached to the firewall policy
policy.name: attach_resource_policy

policy.description: The policy JSON to use for the resource policy; required when `create_resource_policy` is `false`
policy.name: resource_policy

policy.description: A map of RAM resource associations for the created firewall policy
policy.name: ram_resource_associations

policy.description: Set of actions to take on a packet if it does not match any stateful rules in the policy. This can only be specified if the policy has a `stateful_engine_options` block with a rule_order value of `STRICT_ORDER`. You can specify one of either or neither values of `aws:drop_strict` or `aws:drop_established`, as well as any combination of `aws:alert_strict` and `aws:alert_established`
policy.name: stateful_default_actions

policy.description: A list of IAM actions allowed in the resource policy
policy.name: resource_policy_actions

rule-group.description: A list of IAM principals allowed in the resource policy
rule-group.name: resource_policy_principals

rule-group.description: Controls if Network Firewall resources should be created
rule-group.name: create

rule-group.description: The maximum number of operating resources that this rule group can use. For a stateless rule group, the capacity required is the sum of the capacity requirements of the individual rules. For a stateful rule group, the minimum capacity required is the number of individual rules
rule-group.name: capacity

rule-group.description: A friendly description of the rule group
rule-group.name: description

rule-group.description: A friendly name of the rule group
rule-group.name: name

rule-group.description: A configuration block that defines the rule group rules. Required unless `rules` is specified
rule-group.name: rule_group

rule-group.description: A map of RAM resource associations for the created rule group
rule-group.name: ram_resource_associations

rule-group.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
rule-group.name: tags

rule-group.description: The stateful rule group rules specifications in Suricata file format, with one rule per line. Use this to import your existing Suricata compatible rule groups. Required unless `rule_group` is specified
rule-group.name: rules

rule-group.description: Controls if a resource policy should be attached to the rule group
rule-group.name: attach_resource_policy

rule-group.description: KMS encryption configuration settings
rule-group.name: encryption_configuration

rule-group.description: Whether the rule group is stateless (containing stateless rules) or stateful (containing stateful rules). Valid values include: `STATEFUL` or `STATELESS`
rule-group.name: type

rule-group.description: The policy JSON to use for the resource policy; required when `create_resource_policy` is `false`
rule-group.name: resource_policy
